title: Problem Solving: Model Multiplication
essential_question: How can you use the strategy draw a picture to solve equal group problems?
strategies: Draw a picture to show equal groups
Use bar models to represent multiplication
Write a multiplication sentence to solve

pretest.question: There are 5 shelves with 6 video games each. How many games in all?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.4 'Problem Solving: Model Multiplication' pp.115-118
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic B — Equal Groups: Drawing and Modeling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Draw Circles and Dots to Model Equal Groups
learn.content: Draw circles for groups, then put dots inside for items. 4 groups of 3: draw 4 circles, place 3 dots in each. Count all dots: 12. Write the multiplication sentence: 4 × 3 = 12.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.4 'Problem Solving: Model Multiplication' pp.115-118
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic B — Equal Groups: Drawing and Modeling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Bar Models for Multiplication
learn.content: A bar model is a rectangle divided into equal sections. Each section = the size of one group. The number of sections = the number of groups. Add all sections to find the total (the product).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.4 'Problem Solving: Model Multiplication' pp.115-118
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic B — Equal Groups: Drawing and Modeling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Two-Step Problems: Multiply Then Add or Subtract
learn.content: Some problems need two steps. Example: 3 bags of 5 apples, eat 2. Step 1: 3 × 5 = 15 apples. Step 2: 15 − 2 = 13 left. Always multiply first to find the group total, then do the second operation.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.4 'Problem Solving: Model Multiplication' pp.115-118
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic B — Equal Groups: Drawing and Modeling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Choosing the Right Model
learn.content: For equal groups → circles and dots. For side-by-side groups → arrays. For 'how many total in parts' → bar models. All three models show the same multiplication, just drawn differently. Pick the one that fits the story.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.4 'Problem Solving: Model Multiplication' pp.115-118
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic B — Equal Groups: Drawing and Modeling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Reading Word Problems for Modeling
learn.content: Look for these clues: 'each' (tells the group size), 'groups of / rows of / bags of' (tells the number of groups), 'how many in all / total' (asks for the product). Underline the clue words before drawing.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.4 'Problem Solving: Model Multiplication' pp.115-118
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic B — Equal Groups: Drawing and Modeling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 단계별 그리기 — 동그라미와 점
learn.content: 워드프로블럼을 그림으로 옮기는 4단계: 1) 그룹 수만큼 동그라미를 그리기 (예: 4 boxes → 4개 동그라미). 2) 'each' 뒤의 숫자만큼 각 동그라미 안에 점 찍기. 3) 모든 점을 세거나 곱셈 식 쓰기. 4) 답에 단위 적기 (apples, students 등). 흔한 실수: 'each' 키워드를 놓치고 그룹/크기를 뒤바꿈.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.4 'Problem Solving: Model Multiplication' pp.115-118
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic B — Equal Groups: Drawing and Modeling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 바 모델 — 칸과 칸 값
learn.content: 바 모델은 길쭉한 직사각형을 같은 크기의 칸으로 나눕니다. 칸 개수 = 그룹 수, 한 칸의 값 = 한 그룹의 크기. 전체 바의 길이 = 곱(product). 활용 팁: 미지수 문제에 강함. 예: '7칸 바의 전체가 35' → 한 칸 = ?, 7 × ? = 35 → ? = 5.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.4 'Problem Solving: Model Multiplication' pp.115-118
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic B — Equal Groups: Drawing and Modeling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 두 단계 문제 — 곱셈 먼저, 그 다음에
learn.content: '몇 개씩 N묶음, 그 후 ±M' 패턴은 항상 곱셈 먼저, 그 다음 ±M. 1단계: 그룹 총합 계산 (G × S). 2단계: 추가/제거된 양만큼 더하거나 빼기. 예: '3 trays × 5 cookies, 2 burned' → 3×5=15, 15−2=13. 흔한 실수: G+M을 먼저 곱하기 (3×5−2 vs (3×5)−2=13). 괄호처럼 곱셈을 먼저 묶어서 생각하세요.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.4 'Problem Solving: Model Multiplication' pp.115-118
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic B — Equal Groups: Drawing and Modeling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Which picture model correctly shows 3 × 6?
choices: C. Both A and B — they give the same product
try.explanation: A shows 6 × 3 = 18. B shows 3 × 6 = 18. Both equal 18 — same product.

feedback.incorrect: 6 groups of 3 AND 3 groups of 6 both equal 18.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 803 − 256.
practice_r2.explanation: 803−256: 3−6 borrow → 13−6=7; tens 0−5 borrow → 10−6=4 (after lending); hundreds 7−2=5. Result: 547.
hints: Borrow across the zero in the tens place.

practice_r3.question: A student draws a model for 7 × 4. Her partner draws a model for 4 × 7. Both say the product is 28. Are they both right?
practice_r3.explanation: 7 × 4 = 28 and 4 × 7 = 28. The commutative property means factor order doesn't change the product.
